channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABNT20 KNHC 231742
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
200 PM EDT Thu Jul 23 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Bertha, located inland near the Louisiana/Texas border.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

$$
Forecaster D. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Depression Bertha (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...BERTHA WEAKENS TO A DEPRESSION... ...TROPICAL STORM WARNINGS DISCONTINUED... As of 7:00 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 the center of Bertha was located near 29.9, -95.3 with movement W at 13 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1007 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 35 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Bertha
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...BERTHA WEAKENS TO A DEPRESSION... ...TROPICAL STORM WARNINGS DISCONTINUED...

item.title: Tropical Depression Bertha Public Advisory Number 18a
item.description:
Issued at 700 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 232339
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Depression Bertha Intermediate Advisory Number 18A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
700 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026
 
...BERTHA WEAKENS TO A DEPRESSION...
...TROPICAL STORM WARNINGS DISCONTINUED...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 700 PM CDT...0000 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...29.9N 95.3W
ABOUT 10 MI...20 KM NNE OF HOUSTON TEXAS
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...35 MPH...55 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...W OR 280 DEGREES AT 13 MPH...20 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1007 MB...29.74 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
The Tropical Storm Warning from the Vermilion/Cameron Parish Line, 
Louisiana, to Sargent, Texas has been discontinued.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect. 

 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 700 PM CDT (0000 UTC), the center of Tropical Depression Bertha 
was located near latitude 29.9 North, longitude 95.3 West.  Bertha 
is moving toward the west near 13 mph (20 km/h), and this general 
motion is expected to continue tonight.
 
Maximum sustained winds have decreased to near 35 mph (55 km/h) 
with higher gusts.  Additional weakening is expected, and Bertha is 
likely to dissipate tonight.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1007 mb (29.74 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
WIND:  Wind gusts to tropical storm force are still possible along 
the upper Texas coast during the next couple of hours.
 
STORM SURGE:  Water levels may remain elevated through the next
high tide cycle along the Louisiana and Texas coastline but should
subside thereafter.
 
RAINFALL: Bertha is expected to produce total rainfall amounts of 1
to 3 inches with isolated totals to 4 inches into tonight along the
Texas Gulf Coast and South Texas. This rainfall may produce isolated
flash flooding, especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Bertha, please see the National Weather Service Storm Total Rainfall
Graphic available at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and
the Flash Flood Risk graphic at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF: Swells generated by Bertha will continue to affect the
Louisiana and Texas coast through tonight.  These swells are
likely to cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions.
Please consult products from your local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 1000 PM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Pasch/Mahoney
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Advisory Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 232035
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  18
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.8N  94.7W AT 23/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 290 DEGREES AT  11 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1006 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  35 KT WITH GUSTS TO  45 KT.
34 KT.......  0NE 120SE 120SW   0NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 29.8N  94.7W AT 23/2100Z
AT 23/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 29.8N  93.9W
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 29.7N  97.2W...POST-TROP/INLAND
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 29.8N  94.7W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 24/0000Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 24/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forecast Discussion Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 400 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026 
000
WTNT42 KNHC 232037
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Storm Bertha Discussion Number  18
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
400 PM CDT Thu Jul 23 2026

Bertha has been moving along the northern Gulf coast much of the
day, and it now appears to be inland over far eastern Texas.  The
storm remains very asymmetric, with nearly all of the deep
convection over the southwest quadrant, which is now moving inland
over Texas.  An ASCAT-C pass from a few hours ago suggest that the
maximum winds have decreased.  Based on that data, surface
observations, and its degraded appearance in satellite images, the
initial wind speed is lowered to 35 kt.
 
The poor organization of Bertha makes it hard to compute its
initial motion.  In fact, the storm is becoming increasingly
elongated and might be opening up into a trough. Our best guess of
the initial motion is west-northwestward at 11 kt.  However. the
global models suggest that the center could reform again to the
south during the next few hours.  Regardless, Bertha is expected to
move across eastern Texas this evening, and it forecast to
dissipate tonight or on Friday.
 
 
Key Messages:
 
1. Tropical storm conditions are forecast in portions of the
warning area in Texas and Louisiana through the evening.
 
2. Isolated flash flooding remains possible along the Texas Gulf
Coast and South Texas into tonight.
 
3. Coastal flooding along the northern Gulf coast is expected to
gradually subside.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  23/2100Z 29.8N  94.7W   35 KT  40 MPH
 12H  24/0600Z 29.7N  97.2W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/INLAND
 24H  24/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi/R. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Wind Speed Probabilities Number 18
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 232036
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  18           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
2100 UTC THU JUL 23 2026                                             
                                                          
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM BERTHA WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 29.8 NORTH...LONGITUDE 94.7 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 35 KTS...40 MPH...65 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z THU AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       18Z THU 06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 18Z SAT 18Z SUN 18Z MON
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z FRI 18Z FRI 06Z SAT 18Z SAT 18Z SUN 18Z MON 18Z TUE
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
CAMERON LA     34 76   X(76)   X(76)   X(76)   X(76)   X(76)   X(76)
 
PORT ARTHUR TX 34  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
GALVESTON TX   34 89   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)
 
HOUSTON TX     34 26   X(26)   X(26)   X(26)   X(26)   X(26)   X(26)
 
FREEPORT TX    34 77   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)   X(77)
 
GFMX 280N 950W 34  3   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34 70   X(70)   X(70)   X(70)   X(70)   X(70)   X(70)
 
MATAGORDA TX   34 25   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)   X(25)
 
PORT O CONNOR  34  8   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)   X( 8)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
